_readme: Curated local dictionary used by @Intake's lookup_herb tool. Keys are lowercase, underscore-separated common/Ayurvedic names. 'active_compounds' lists the constituent names used as lookup keys into docking_lookup.json.

guggulu.traditional_use: Lipid-lowering and anti-inflammatory resin extract, commonly used in Ayurveda for hyperlipidemia and joint conditions.

yashtimadhu.traditional_use: Demulcent root used for cough, acid reflux, and as a general adaptogen/rasayana.

karela.traditional_use: Traditional anti-diabetic vegetable/juice used to support healthy blood glucose levels.

st_johns_wort.traditional_use: Western herbal supplement widely sold for mood support; included here for its well-documented cross-system interaction profile relevant to Indian transplant patients who may self-medicate with imported supplements.

tulsi.traditional_use: Widely consumed adaptogen/rasayana herb for respiratory and immune support, often taken as tea.

ashwagandha.traditional_use: Widely used Ayurvedic rasayana adaptogen taken for stress, fatigue, and general vitality enhancement.

brahmi.traditional_use: Classical Ayurvedic medhya (cognitive-enhancing) herb used for memory improvement and anxiety reduction.

arjuna.traditional_use: Bark of Terminalia arjuna used in Ayurveda as a cardioprotective tonic for heart failure, angina, and dyslipidemia.

neem.traditional_use: Pan-Indian medicinal tree; leaves and bark used for skin conditions, blood purification, antimicrobial effects, and diabetes management.

black_pepper.traditional_use: Culinary spice and Ayurvedic bioavailability enhancer; piperine is used in supplement formulations (BioPerine) to enhance absorption of other compounds.

fenugreek.traditional_use: Widely consumed Ayurvedic anti-diabetic and galactagogue; seeds are taken as powder, soaked, or in cooking.

shankhpushpi.traditional_use: Ayurvedic medhya herb used for cognitive enhancement, insomnia, and anxiety; often prepared as a syrup or powder.

garlic.traditional_use: Universal culinary and medicinal plant used for cardiovascular health, antimicrobial effects, and immune support.

shatavari.traditional_use: Female health tonic in Ayurveda; used for hormonal balance, fertility support, and as a galactagogue.

dandelion.traditional_use: Traditional diuretic and 'liver tonic' herb; dandelion leaf has natriuretic properties, root is used as a prebiotic.

turmeric.traditional_use: Ubiquitous Indian spice and Ayurvedic anti-inflammatory agent; taken as cooking spice, milk (haldi doodh), and curcumin extract supplements.

ginger.traditional_use: Widely used Ayurvedic and culinary herb for nausea, inflammation, and joint pain; consumed as fresh root, dried powder, or supplement capsules.

ginkgo_biloba.traditional_use: Western and TCM supplement for cognitive enhancement, circulation, and tinnitus; standardized extracts (EGb 761) are widely imported and used in India.

valerian.traditional_use: Western herbal sedative used for insomnia and anxiety; popular as a 'natural sleep aid' supplement increasingly imported and used in India.
